name: Arkansas Contractors Licensing Board Licensed Contractor Search
updateFrequency: unknown; verify against the official contractor search before relying on freshness assumptions
licenseTypesIncluded: Commercial contractor licenses and subcontractor registrations
Residential specialty and home improvement licenses
knownExclusions: This source is scoped to the Arkansas Contractors Licensing Board and does not represent every local permit, business registration, or trade credential in Arkansas.

Some contractor classifications require a separate Arkansas trade credential, including master electrician, master plumber, HVACR, asbestos, fire-protection, or underground-storage credentials; this source does not replace those issuing authorities.
The public lookup's inactive and historical coverage remains unverified, and no machine-readable export has been confirmed.
rateLimitNotes: No adapter is implemented. Prefer official bulk options if discovered before considering lookup automation.
publicRecordsNotes: Official lookup source metadata only. Absence from this source is not proof that a license or registration does not exist elsewhere.
officialBulkDownloadNotes: No official bulk download has been confirmed in this phase.
researchNotes: The board and its Residential Contractors Committee regulate commercial contractors, residential builders and remodelers, residential specialties, home improvement contractors, and residential roofer registrations. The April 2026 commercial rules enumerate broad and specialty classifications across all tracked trade domains. Companion trade credentials remain required where the rules say so, and lookup automation remains blocked pending a stable reviewed interface.
maintainerNotes: Keep the contractor-board scope and companion trade-license limitations explicit. The classification evidence supports board coverage, not a claim that this lookup alone proves every credential required for the work.

researchEvidence.note: Official public lookup and current access shape reviewed.
Official April 2026 commercial law and rules reviewed for contractor classifications and companion credential requirements.
Official residential law and rules reviewed for builder, remodeler, specialty, and home improvement scope.

blocker.summary: No stable, documented machine-readable official interface is currently suitable for an offline-tested adapter.
